name: Rocky Mountain Oysters on the Half Shell
recipeInstructions:
aka: calf fries, Rocky Mountain Oysters (sheep or turkey testicles may be used also)
pure hog lard is the best, but a mixture of 60% peanut oil and 40% vegetable oil will do
1. Split the tough skin-like muscle that surrounds each "oyster" with a very sharp knife.
2. Remove the skin from the "oysters."
3. Place "oysters" into a pan with enough salt water to cover them for one hour (this removes some of the blood).
4. Drain the "oysters."
5. Transfer "oysters" to a large pot and add enough water to float the "oysters" along with a generous tablespoon of vinegar.
6. Parboil until cooled, then rinse.
7. Slice each "oyster" into 1/4 inch thick ovals.
8. Sprinkle salt and pepper on both sides of sliced "oysters" to taste.
9. Mix flour, cornmeal, and garlic powder in a bowl.
10. Roll each "oyster" slice into the dry mixture.
11. Dip into milk.
12. Dip into the dry mixture again.
13. Dip into wine quickly (repeat if a thicker crust is desired).
14. Place each "oyster" into hot cooking oil.
15. Add Louisiana Hot Sauce to the cooking oil and cook until golden brown or tender, using a wire mesh strainer to remove when finished.
16. Serve in cardboard beer cartons, layered with paper towels. Eat immediately!
17. Variations: Serve cooked "oysters" on an oyster half shell with a sprig of parsley and a few drops of lemon juice.
